The Island is a wonderful place to live but it has been badly let down by the Tory Government and the previous Labour local council and Sadiq Khan who have encouraged massive overdevelopment. This has put unbearable burdens on local infrastructure and even affected water pressure.

Meanwhile the current Tower Hamlets Mayor Lutfur Rahman talks a good game but does nothing. He has a terrible record of causing division in our community and the current Labour MP is aligned with him.

So much of what is done to the Island has not benefitted local residents. Whether it is local jobs, housing or transport. Meanwhile the sitting Labour MP plays racially divisive politics whilst failing to represent her constituents. We need change in Poplar and Limehouse. We need Reform.

Ibrahim is your current councillor as well as being a local businessman. He has been instrumental in many success stories across Waterloo and South Bank. This includes securing funding for much needed improvements, supporting families and helping local businesses throughout the pandemic. Through his hard work and determination, he works to maximise the potential of our local area and make it the best possible place to live.

We don’t pause often enough to ask ourselves whether the things that impact our daily lives are working for us. Health services. Housing. Schools. Transport. The Council. Are they worklng for you?

I decided to run for Lambeth Council because as a resident of Lambeth and, in particular, as a parent I too frequently sees how “the system” works against residents just trying to get on with the everyday and live a good life.

My three priorities as a councillor in Waterloo & South Bank will be:
- to make it easier for constituents to access the Council/public services they need;
- to radically improve the maintenance of housing estates in the ward and improve conditions for all categories of renters;
- to seek enhancements to the issues that most affect the community’s day-to-day life such as accessible pavements, road safety, cleanliness, open spaces, policing and personal safety.

I also take a special interest in getting the support system for our young people with special education needs (SEN) and their families working better. I will commit to making contact with all families in the ward with an Education, Health & Care Plan (EHCP) or diagnosis and hold SEN surgeries so we can work together with families to remove obstacles and improve the way things work.

Originally from Essex, I moved to Lambeth over 20 years ago. I have two sons with autism at schools in the borough and work for a cultural charity. In 2022, I was 67 votes short of being elected as a councillor for Waterloo & South Bank.

I’m Oliviero, a 19-year-old architecture student and lifelong local. Being born, growing up, and literally taking my first steps here, I know what makes our community special.
As former Co-Chair of the Lambeth Youth Council and Youth Officer for Rush Common and St Martins, I already have a track record of fighting for our residents. With my Italian and Jamaican heritage, I proudly represent the vibrant diversity of our borough.

I’m standing to bring fresh energy and genuine commitment to the council and residents, ensuring our housing and public spaces truly work for everyone. Let’s build a better Lambeth together.

Lambeth Labour has let us down. They say there isn't money to keep our streets clean, keep our streetlights bright or to perform basic repairs to housing.

In 2019 I moved to Waterloo and South Bank as a student at King's, and in 2024 I first realised how awful the council was, as I dealt with an 11-month long leak in my flat on a council estate. Sadly, my story is in no way unique.

It's time for a change in Waterloo. Labour can't keep taking us for granted.

Lib Dems run councils better. In Lib Dem-run Sutton, we're building new council homes. Lib Dems listen to residents and work hard for their communities. We stand against division, and we get stuff done. We're problem fixers, not finger-pointers.

Only the Lib Dems can beat Labour in this election, having come just 67 votes behind them in 2022. It would be an honour to serve you as your local councillor, and Doug and I are ready to get stuck in.

Together, we can fix Lambeth.

Celeste Hicks is a northerner who has chosen to settle in London, and has lived in Herne Hill for 3 years with her husband and children. The two older children attend a local primary school. Celeste loves the strong sense of community, the views and the beautiful green spaces of Herne Hill.

I am a retired nurse and midwife, having worked in palliative care for the last 20 years and prior to that as a midwife at Guys and St Thomas'. I’ve been active in the Green Party in Lambeth for about 5 years and love being involved in a campaign which has a well-thought-out strategy, realistic goals and is slowly but surely achieving them.

I also spend a lot of time and energy on campaigning for the NHS, locally and nationally - for more resources and on fighting back encroaching privatisation, and for more pay for NHS workers - so I’m still active in my union as a retired member.

Tom Palmer is an activist for cleaner air, safer streets, and neighbourhood representation.

“Leading a number of community organisations, I know how effective engagement, communication and transparency can be to achieve vital improvements and better services. I want to see our streets safer, cleaner, greener and more child friendly and will work with residents and businesses towards those outcomes.”

I’ve worked for charities campaigning on UK child poverty, climate and economic justice. I have children in local state schools and am passionate about ensuring West Dulwich is a neighbourhood with opportunities for all children, safe for women and young people and a tidier, pleasant environment for all with less pollution, safer cycling and free of flytipping.

Stacey Young is a local community champion who’s been working to stand up for local residents.

“Living on the Elmworth Grove estate, I’ve seen how residents get ignored by Lambeth Council. They just don’t listen to us – on home repairs, roads, health, and more. I’m standing for the Greens so we can make sure that residents are at the centre of every decision. We deserve a cleaner, fairer, Greener Lambeth!”

I’m Aidan Stimson and I'm proud to be standing as your West Dulwich Labour and Co-operative candidate.

Originally from Australia, I've called Lambeth home for over a decade. I know West Dulwich well - as a local resident and former caseworker for Helen Hayes MP.

From my work and life experiences, I believe that I would make a great local councillor for the residents in West Dulwich. I developed a unique skill set, which
includes:

- Dealing with casework and surgeries in Lambeth
- Assisting with community meetings
- Listening to various concerns and advocating on behalf of residents
- Detailed knowledge of West Dulwich, both from living locally and working closely with previous councillors and my running partner Judith Cavanagh.

I now work for Ed Miliband MP as his Parliamentary Assistant, helping to deliver for his constituents in Doncaster North.

I have a young family, and I care deeply for our environment, our community and neighbourhood, and I will do my best to deliver for our ward.

If elected, I will build on the excellent work of Fred Cowell, and work closely with Judith Cavanagh to be a visible, accessible councillor who listens and champions West Dulwich every step of the way.

Like you, I recently received my latest Council Tax bill. Unsurprisingly, it had gone up by just shy of 5%, climbing to over £1,500 a year for a property like mine. Like you, I want our contributions to Lambeth’s finances to provide our area with effective services. In spite of another heavy tax increase, we all know the services on which we all rely will not get better. Instead, under Labour control, Lambeth will continue to deliver poor services. We deserve better.

This by-election is a chance for you to have your say on how Lambeth Labour has run Herne Hill and Loughborough Junction. Talking to people across the ward, there’s a lot to say. Whether you are worried about crime, the fate of local businesses, the condition of your street, how Lambeth has managed your home or the repeated damage to Brockwell Park, you want to send them a message.

Lambeth Labour is not working for Herne Hill and Loughborough Junction. The current Lib Dem and Green opposition on Lambeth Council is not working for Herne Hill and Loughborough Junction. Things will only get worse if the current situation continues. We cannot let that happen.

I’m standing in this by-election because I want things to get better for Herne Hill and Loughborough Junction. I want safer streets, I want local shops to stay open, I want the pavements cleared of weeds and rubbish, I want social tenants not to have to live in mould infested homes and I want to use our parks in the summer. A vote for me as your Conservative candidate is a vote to say you want things to get better, not worse.

Please lend me your vote on 1st May.

Labour won nearly all the seats in Lambeth in 2022. Whatever your political affiliation, you may agree having one party dominate in this way leads to complacency , taking residents for granted. That is precisely what we have seen in our ward. The most obvious example is the huge waste of money introducing a traffic scheme without proper consultation.

I am standing as a Liberal Democrat candidate, as they are the party with the strongest principles – a commitment to doing what is right and listening to the views of residents. But I am appealing to all those who , regardless of party affiliation, simply want to see transparent, competent and efficient local government. I have worked in a City law firm for many years, negotiating major complex projects and resolving difficult disputes. I know how to get things done, listening carefully to clients. I can do the same for the residents of our beloved West Dulwich, where I have lived for over 40 years .

I should be delighted if you could lend me the opportunity to represent your interests.

With over 40 years of experience in Financial
Services, I am standing because I'm determined to
challenge, using common sense, all of the council
priorities.
Lambeth is over £1.1 billion in debt but continues
to spend millions on imposed vanity projects
driven by pure ideology. This has to stop!
